code:
from pyspark.ml.classification import LogisticRegression
lr = LogisticRegression(maxIter=10, regParam=0.3, elasticNetParam=0.8)
model = lr.fit(training_df)
predictions = model.transform(test_df)
root_cause: PySpark ML LogisticRegression fit/transform pattern is not supported in Snowpark Connect (SPRKCNTPY2500). The pyspark.ml module is not available. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.linear_model import LogisticRegression; model = LogisticRegression(max_iter=10); model.fit(training_df); predictions = model.predict(test_df). Note param name changes: maxIter→max_iter, regParam→C (inverse), elasticNetParam→l1_ratio. transform()→predict().

code:
from pyspark.ml.classification import RandomForestClassifier
rf = RandomForestClassifier(numTrees=100, maxDepth=5, labelCol="label", featuresCol="features")
model = rf.fit(training_df)
root_cause: PySpark ML RandomForestClassifier is not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.ensemble import RandomForestClassifier; rf = RandomForestClassifier(n_estimators=100, max_depth=5, input_cols=[...], label_cols=[...]). Param mapping: numTrees→n_estimators, maxDepth→max_depth, featuresCol→input_cols (list), labelCol→label_cols (list).

gbt = GBTClassifier(maxIter=10, maxDepth=5, stepSize=0.1)

root_cause: PySpark ML GBTClassifier not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.ensemble import GradientBoostingClassifier; or use XGBoost: from snowflake.ml.modeling.xgboost import XGBClassifier. Param mapping: maxIter→n_estimators, maxDepth→max_depth, stepSize→learning_rate.

code:
from pyspark.ml.classification import DecisionTreeClassifier
dt = DecisionTreeClassifier(maxDepth=5, labelCol="label", featuresCol="features")

root_cause: PySpark ML DecisionTreeClassifier not available in Snowpark Connect. code:
from pyspark.ml.regression import LinearRegression
lr = LinearRegression(maxIter=10, regParam=0.3, elasticNetParam=0.8, labelCol="price")
model = lr.fit(training_df)
print(f"RMSE: {model.summary.rootMeanSquaredError}")
root_cause: PySpark ML LinearRegression not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.linear_model import LinearRegression. model.summary is not available — use snowflake.ml.modeling.metrics for evaluation: mean_squared_error(y_true, y_pred). Param mapping: regParam→alpha, elasticNetParam→l1_ratio.

code:
from pyspark.ml import Pipeline
from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, VectorAssembler
from pyspark.ml.classification import LogisticRegression
pipeline = Pipeline(stages=[indexer, assembler, lr])
model = pipeline.fit(training_df)
predictions = model.transform(test_df)
root_cause: PySpark ML Pipeline chains multiple stages (Transformers + Estimators). Not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML Pipeline: from snowflake.ml.modeling.pipeline import Pipeline. Note: Snowflake ML Pipeline uses sklearn-compatible API — stages must implement fit/transform. VectorAssembler has no direct equivalent — Snowflake ML handles multi-column input via input_cols parameter.

code:
from pyspark.ml.tuning import CrossValidator, ParamGridBuilder
paramGrid = ParamGridBuilder().addGrid(lr.maxIter, [10, 100]).addGrid(lr.regParam, [0.1, 0.01]).build()
cv = CrossValidator(estimator=pipeline, estimatorParamMaps=paramGrid, evaluator=evaluator, numFolds=3)
bestModel = cv.fit(training_df)
root_cause: PySpark ML CrossValidator with ParamGridBuilder is not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.model_selection import GridSearchCV; gs = GridSearchCV(estimator=model, param_grid={'max_iter': [10, 100], 'C': [0.1, 0.01]}, cv=3); gs.fit(training_df). API follows sklearn conventions.

code:
from pyspark.ml.feature import VectorAssembler
assembler = VectorAssembler(inputCols=["age", "salary", "experience"], outputCol="features")
assembled_df = assembler.transform(df)
lr = LogisticRegression(featuresCol="features", labelCol="label")
root_cause: VectorAssembler combines multiple columns into a single vector column for ML input. Not needed in Snowpark Connect — Snowflake ML models accept input_cols as a list directly: lr = LogisticRegression(input_cols=["age", "salary", "experience"], label_cols=["label"]). Remove VectorAssembler entirely from the pipeline.

code:
from pyspark.ml.feature import StringIndexer, OneHotEncoder
indexer = StringIndexer(inputCol="category", outputCol="category_index")
encoder = OneHotEncoder(inputCol="category_index", outputCol="category_vec")
indexed_df = indexer.fit(df).transform(df)
root_cause: PySpark StringIndexer/OneHotEncoder for categorical encoding. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.preprocessing import OrdinalEncoder, OneHotEncoder. Snowflake ML encoders work directly on DataFrame columns: encoder = OneHotEncoder(input_cols=["category"], output_cols=["category_encoded"]); encoder.fit(df).transform(df).

code:
from pyspark.ml.feature import StandardScaler
scaler = StandardScaler(inputCol="features", outputCol="scaled_features", withStd=True, withMean=True)
scaler_model = scaler.fit(assembled_df)
scaled_df = scaler_model.transform(assembled_df)
root_cause: PySpark StandardScaler for feature normalization. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.preprocessing import StandardScaler; scaler = StandardScaler(input_cols=["age", "salary"], output_cols=["age_scaled", "salary_scaled"]); scaler.fit(df).transform(df). Works on individual columns — no VectorAssembler needed.

code:
from pyspark.ml.evaluation import BinaryClassificationEvaluator, MulticlassClassificationEvaluator
evaluator = BinaryClassificationEvaluator(labelCol="label", rawPredictionCol="rawPrediction", metricName="areaUnderROC")
auc = evaluator.evaluate(predictions)
root_cause: PySpark ML Evaluators not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML metrics: from snowflake.ml.modeling.metrics import accuracy_score, precision_score, recall_score, f1_score, log_loss, mean_squared_error. Usage: accuracy = accuracy_score(df=predictions, y_true_col_names="label", y_pred_col_names="prediction").

code:
from pyspark.ml.clustering import KMeans
kmeans = KMeans(k=3, seed=42, featuresCol="features")
model = kmeans.fit(assembled_df)
centers = model.clusterCenters()
root_cause: PySpark ML KMeans clustering not available in Snowpark Connect. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.cluster import KMeans; km = KMeans(n_clusters=3, random_state=42, input_cols=["col1", "col2"]); km.fit(df). Param mapping: k→n_clusters, seed→random_state. clusterCenters()→cluster_centers_ attribute.

code:
from pyspark.ml.recommendation import ALS
als = ALS(maxIter=10, regParam=0.1, userCol="userId", itemCol="movieId", ratingCol="rating")
model = als.fit(ratings_df)
recommendations = model.recommendForAllUsers(10)
root_cause: PySpark ML ALS (collaborative filtering) not available in Snowpark Connect. No direct Snowflake ML equivalent for matrix factorization recommendations. Options: 1) Use Snowflake Cortex ML functions for simpler predictions, 2) Train ALS model externally and register in Snowflake Model Registry, 3) Implement as a Python stored procedure with numpy/scipy.

code:
from pyspark.ml.feature import PCA
pca = PCA(k=3, inputCol="features", outputCol="pca_features")
pca_model = pca.fit(assembled_df)
pca_df = pca_model.transform(assembled_df)
root_cause: PySpark ML PCA for dimensionality reduction. Replace with Snowflake ML: from snowflake.ml.modeling.decomposition import PCA; pca = PCA(n_components=3, input_cols=["col1", "col2", "col3"], output_cols=["pc1", "pc2", "pc3"]); pca.fit(df).transform(df). Param mapping: k→n_components.

PySpark ML model.save()/Model.load() uses HDFS/cloud storage. Replace with Snowflake Model Registry: from snowflake.ml.registry import Registry; reg = Registry(session); reg.log_model(model, model_name="my_model", ...); loaded = reg.get_model("my_model").default. Model Registry provides versioning, lineage, and deployment.

code:
from pyspark.mllib.classification import LogisticRegressionWithLBFGS
from pyspark.mllib.regression import LabeledPoint
from pyspark.mllib.linalg import Vectors
data = [LabeledPoint(1.0, Vectors.dense([1.0, 2.0]))]
model = LogisticRegressionWithLBFGS.train(sc.parallelize(data))
root_cause: pyspark.mllib (RDD-based ML, legacy) is not supported in Snowpark Connect (SPRKCNTPY3000). This is the older ML library that operates on RDDs. Replace with Snowflake ML (snowflake.ml.modeling.*) which provides sklearn-compatible API. LabeledPoint/Vectors have no equivalent — use DataFrames directly.
